reviews.review_msg: Ryan is a really passionate and engaging instructor. It's clear he is very knowledgeable about policing and the lectures were great even though they were three hours long. He broke down course concepts well and set clear guidelines for the assignments.
Great class, even better person. Highly highly recommend Ryan!!

Great class. Show up and take notes  and the exams are fine. Ryan goes through the readings and goes into detail. Brought police officers out, did an online interview, even made a simulation to show the dispatch process!

Easily one of the best crim prof at SFU. Literally attend lectures and you are guaranteed an easy B, no textbook just articles which he goes over in class. Very passionate prof and he knows what he's talking about. Highly recommend taking this class, especially intro to policing.

Gradingharsh, 90% is A. must type very fast to keep up with heavy lectures and his speed. exam written answers must be verbatim from slides, not your own wording. The course feels like memorization, not real learning, hard with 40pgs notes. TA grading marked wrong. Police panels are 1 hour of taking notes, tested only on what officers say out loud.

Intro to policing with Ryan was awesome. He doesn't use a textbook, just a few readings every week. He's engaging and really passionate about the topic. Legit tells you whats on the exams and what to study for. Highly recommend!!

He was cool, the content wasn't difficult and the exams were fair. However, his grading for the exams was not. His answer keys to written Qs were extremely limited and did not allow anything other than verbatim answers, even if you stated the correct answer?? The TA wasn't the best while marking too, always review your exam!!

Ryan is one of the best profs I've ever had. Super straight up, approachable guy that genuinely wants you to succeed. Attendance is mandatory but he keeps everyone engaged during the lectures. He lays out what you need to do to succeed in the class and doesn't try to confuse you. Exam material is directly reflective of the lectures and readings.

Took intro to policing and it was super informative being taught by someone who went through the process of becoming a police officer. He is great at making it make sense and if you don't understand he has no problem breaking it down further. He is also very approachable and tries his best to set students up with success!
Cool, hockey dude, should post slides.
